name: initializer
summary: Module-level boot class that contributes DI bindings, controllers, listeners, and more.
description: Creates an initializer class with DI bindings
problem: Your application is growing past a single bootstrap and you want a dedicated initializer for a domain (payments, billing, integrations) that owns the bindings, controllers, listeners, and other contributions for that area.
appliesWhen: A new domain or feature module is being added to the application
You want bindings and contributions for that domain grouped in one place
Other recipes in this domain will register against this initializer
You're refactoring a sprawling AppInit into per-domain initializers
avoidWhen: The application is still small enough that AppInit is fine
You're adding one or two bindings — extend an existing initializer instead
You don't have enough domain logic to justify a module
examples: Add an initializer for the payments module
Create a module bootstrap for the integrations area
I need a per-domain initializer for the orders feature
tradeoffs: One class with empty contribution methods. Useful when contributions accumulate; overkill when they don't. Other recipes will modify it via AST mutations — that's the design.
outputs: Empty Has* contribution methods to be extended by other recipes
postApply: Wire the initializer into your Application's boot sequence
Use other recipes (listener, controller, datastore, etc.) to add contributions — they will modify this class via AST mutation
Verify via `phpnomad inspect:di` that the initializer is part of the boot graph

name.aiHint: PascalCase ending in 'Initializer' or 'Init'. Name describes the domain it owns (PaymentsInitializer, BillingInit).
